senses.definition: to move away from a person, thing, or situation

examples.x: The hiker encountered a bear and wisely chose to back off slowly.
The dog growled, warning everyone to back off.
The crowd was asked to back off to give emergency responders room to work.
The firefighter was forced to back off when the flames intensified.
The cyclist decided to back off from the busy intersection to avoid a potential collision.

senses.definition: to take something or someone away from a place or situation and move them to a different one

examples.x: They want to bear off the old furniture from the room.
The movers will bear off the furniture from our old house to the new one.
The delivery service will bear off the package from the warehouse to your doorstep.
Let's bear off the old files from the office and store them in the archive.

senses.definition: to forcefully make someone or something leave by chasing after them threateningly

examples.x: The security guard chased off the trespassers from the construction site.
The mother hen bravely chased off the fox that approached the chicken coop.
The farmer had to chase off the crows to protect his crops.
The security guard chased off the trespassers from the construction site.
As soon as the meeting concluded, he had to dash off to catch a train.
The urgent phone call required him to dash off from the office and attend to a family emergency.
Upon hearing the news, she had to dash off from the gathering to deal with a personal matter.
Noticing the approaching storm, the beachgoers decided to dash off before it started raining.
Feeling unwell, she had to dash off from the party without saying goodbye to anyone.

senses.definition: to fall from a particular position to the ground

examples.x: The clumsy cat tried to balance on the narrow ledge but eventually lost its footing and fell off.
Carelessly perched on the edge, the hat started to fall off with each gust of wind.
He fell off and scraped his knee while cycling.

senses.definition: to leave a bus, train, airplane, etc.

examples.x: The passengers were asked to get off the bus at the next stop.
He managed to get off the sinking boat just in time.
She got off the bus at the next stop.
The passengers will get off the train at the station.
He was the last one to get off the subway at the final station.

senses.definition: to leave one's spouse or partner to pursue a romantic relationship with someone else

examples.x: She decided to go off with her co-worker, leaving her husband heartbroken.
After years of marriage, he went off with his high school sweetheart, leaving his wife devastated.
The scandal erupted when he went off with his wife's best friend.
They both went off with each other, leaving their respective partners behind.
The film tells the story of a woman who goes off with a stranger she met while traveling, leaving her old life behind.

senses.definition: (of a spacecraft or aircraft) to leave the ground, particularly vertically

examples.x: The rocket lifted off with a thunderous roar, marking the beginning of the historic mission.
The tension was palpable as the countdown reached zero and the aircraft lifted off, soaring into the sky.
The airplane taxied down the runway, its engines gaining power, and then lifted off smoothly into the clear blue sky.
The spectators watched in awe as the helicopter lifted off, its rotors spinning rapidly, ready to transport the injured hiker to safety.
The small experimental aircraft lifted off smoothly, its pilot eager to test its capabilities.

senses.definition: to leave quickly, often in order to escape or avoid someone or something

examples.x: The thief made off after robbing the bank.
They managed to make off without paying the bill.
The robbers made off with all the cash.
She grabbed her things and made off before the argument escalated.
He tried to make off with the documents but was caught at the door.

senses.definition: to go somewhere, especially in a hurry or with little preparation

examples.x: She immediately packed off to the hospital after hearing about her friend's accident.
Hearing the news, he packed off to his hometown.
They got a tip about a secret sale and packed off to the mall immediately.
She received a last-minute invitation and packed off to the party.
When he learned about the special event in the city, he just packed off without telling anyone.

senses.definition: to leave somewhere with something that one does not own
to leave quickly or abruptly, often because of an urgent or unexpected situation

examples.x: She had to rush off to catch her flight, leaving the party early.
I'll have to rush off if I want to make it to the meeting on time.
We should rush off before the traffic gets worse during the evening rush hour.
He realized he needed to rush off when he saw the approaching storm.
I might have to rush off from work if my child's school calls with an issue.
After the argument, he shot off from the party without saying a word.
After finishing her work, she shot off to catch the bus.
As soon as the movie ended, they shot off from the cinema.
When the rain started, everyone shot off to find shelter.
As the party ended, they shot off home to beat the traffic.

senses.definition: to swiftly depart from a place, often with the aim of avoiding something or someone

examples.x: He tried to skip off before anyone noticed his mistake at the meeting.
The mischievous child attempted to skip off with a handful of cookies from the jar.
In a rush, she had to skip off from the party to catch the last train home.
The student tried to skip off from class early to avoid the upcoming pop quiz.
Upon hearing the news, she decided to skip off work early to attend the special event.

senses.definition: to leave a place, meeting, or situation without drawing attention to oneself

examples.x: He decided to slide off the party early to avoid saying lengthy goodbyes.
During the boring lecture, she managed to slide off without anyone noticing.
The employee tried to slide off the office without being seen by the boss.
During the break, he managed to slide off the workshop and take a moment to himself.
Not wanting to disturb the atmosphere, they chose to slide off the restaurant after finishing their meal.

senses.definition: to leave a place quietly so that others may not notice one's departure

examples.x: Not wanting to disturb the meeting, she decided to slip off without saying goodbye.
He tried to slip off the party unnoticed to avoid any awkward farewells.
They slipped off the restaurant quietly after finishing their meal to catch a movie.
In the middle of the lecture, she quietly slipped off to attend to an urgent matter.
They planned to slip off the event early, preferring a quiet exit over a crowded departure.

senses.definition: to leave a surface and begin flying

examples.x: The airplane is ready to take off from the runway.
Pilots need permission to take off from the airport.
Birds effortlessly take off into the sky with a flap of their wings.
The rocket ship is designed to take off vertically from the launchpad.
As the helicopter prepared to take off, the rotor blades began to spin.

senses.definition: to move away from a location or situation

examples.x: The employee walked off the stressful situation for a few minutes.
The athlete walked off the field after the intense match.
They walked off the crowded event to find a quieter space.
After the argument, she walked off the conversation, needing some space.
The musician walked off the stage after a mesmerizing performance.

senses.definition: to take something without permission, especially by stealing

examples.x: Despite security measures, someone managed to walk off with the expensive equipment.
Let's not allow anyone to walk off with our belongings in the busy airport.
The thief walked off with the jewelry during the commotion.
The pickpocket skillfully walked off with his wallet in the crowded market.
The mischievous kids walked off with candies from the store.
